children.name: Chleuasicus
children.description: Small, stout parrotbills of Asian bamboo and grasslands, with thick finch-like bills and soft fluffy plumage.

children.name: Chleuasicus atrosuperciliaris
children.description: A delicate, long-tailed parrotbill with a prominent black brow and soft buff body, moving in family groups through bamboo and rich understorey in the eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Conostoma
children.description: The largest of the parrotbills, with a deep, conical bill adapted for crushing seeds and bamboo shoots.

children.name: Conostoma oemodium
children.description: The largest parrotbill, a stout grey-brown bird with a massive orange bill, inhabiting temperate rhododendron and bamboo forests of the Himalayas and adjacent highlands.

children.name: Fulvetta
children.description: Small, active babblers of Asian montane forests, formerly lumped with the larger Alcippe fulvettas but now recognized as part of the sylviid radiation.

children.name: Fulvetta cinereiceps
children.description: A fulvetta with a grey crown and finely streaked white throat, inhabiting montane forests of central and southern China.

children.name: Fulvetta danisi
children.description: A recently described species split from the Spectacled Fulvetta, found in the highlands of northern Vietnam and Laos.

children.name: Fulvetta formosana
children.description: A fulvetta endemic to Taiwan, with a brown crown, strong white supercilium, and greyish ear coverts.

children.name: Fulvetta ludlowi
children.description: A fulvetta of the eastern Himalayas and adjacent Tibet, with a grey head, brown back, and buff underparts.

children.name: Fulvetta manipurensis
children.description: A grey-headed fulvetta with a distinctive white eye-ring and warm brown body, found in the northeastern Indian hills.

children.name: Fulvetta ruficapilla
children.description: A fulvetta with a rich chestnut crown and bold white eye-ring, found in montane broadleaf forests of southern China and northern Indochina.

children.name: Fulvetta striaticollis
children.description: A grey-and-brown fulvetta with a finely streaked throat, endemic to temperate forests of central and western China.

children.name: Fulvetta vinipectus
children.description: A small babbler of Himalayan and Chinese montane forests, with a warm buff throat and breast and conspicuous white brow.

children.name: Lioparus
children.description: A monotypic genus of brilliantly coloured fulvetta, the sole species skulking in montane bamboo thickets from the Himalayas to Southeast Asia.

children.name: Lioparus chrysotis
children.description: A striking fulvetta with a bright golden-yellow throat and breast, skulking in bamboo understorey of Himalayan and Southeast Asian montane forests.

children.name: Moupinia
children.description: A monotypic babbler genus of Chinese scrub, with a long tail and a restless, inquisitive manner.

children.name: Moupinia poecilotis
children.description: A long-tailed, rufous-tinged babbler of Chinese montane scrub, with a distinct white brow and a loud, scolding chatter.

children.name: Myzornis
children.description: A brilliantly coloured monotypic genus of the eastern Himalayas, the Fire-tailed Myzornis gleams like a jewel in rhododendron forests.

children.name: Myzornis pyrrhoura
children.description: A brilliant emerald-green babbler with a crimson tail, black-and-white wing pattern, and a thin curved bill; a sought-after gem of Himalayan rhododendron forests.

children.name: Paradoxornis
children.description: The type genus of the parrotbills, with a remarkable parrot-like bill adapted for splitting bamboo stems and extracting seeds.

children.name: Paradoxornis guttaticollis
children.description: A heavily built, boldly marked parrotbill with a spotted breast and a thick yellow bill, foraging in noisy flocks through bamboo and tall grasses.

children.name: Parisoma
children.description: Slim African warblers of acacia savanna and scrub, formerly grouped with the tit-flycatchers.

children.name: Sylvia boehmi
children.description: A small grey warbler of dry acacia bush in East Africa, marked with a narrow black breast band.

children.name: Sylvia buryi
children.description: A warbler of highland juniper and acacia woodland in southwest Arabia and the Horn of Africa, with a clear piping song.

children.name: Sylvia layardi
children.description: A grey, white-eyed warbler of arid karoo scrub in southern Africa.

children.name: Sylvia lugens
children.description: A rather plain brown warbler of East African highland forest edge and scrub, with an energetic, scratchy song.

children.name: Sylvia subcoerulea
children.description: A grey warbler of southern African acacia savanna and scrub, distinguished by its warm rufous vent and undertail coverts.

children.name: Psittiparus
children.description: Large parrotbills with striking head patterns of rufous, white, and black, foraging in flocks through bamboo and grassland.

children.name: Psittiparus ruficeps
children.description: A striking parrotbill with a rufous crown, black mask, white breast, and thick yellow bill, found in bamboo and scrub of the eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Rhopophilus
children.description: Long-tailed, skulking warblers of Chinese hillsides, their loud metallic songs carrying across scrub-covered slopes.

children.name: Rhopophilus albosuperciliaris
children.description: A recently split relative of the Chinese Hill Warbler, with a broader white supercilium and greyer upperparts, found in montane scrub of central China.

children.name: Rhopophilus pekinensis
children.description: A long-tailed, shy warbler of northern Chinese scrub and hillsides, with a loud, metallic song often given in a brief song flight.

children.name: Sinosuthora
children.description: Compact parrotbills of East Asia with short, deep bills and soft, fluffy plumage, often occurring in large flocks.

children.name: Sinosuthora conspicillata
children.description: A sociable, chestnut-and-grey parrotbill with a prominent white eye-ring, foraging in flocks through bamboo and scrub in central and southern China.

children.name: Sinosuthora webbiana
children.description: A small, brownish parrotbill with a rich vinous throat and breast, one of the commonest and most widespread parrotbills of East Asia.

children.name: Suthora
children.description: Small, active parrotbills of montane bamboo and scrub, with short parrot-like bills and a tendency to hang acrobatically from stems.

children.name: Suthora fulvifrons
children.description: A warm fulvous-brown parrotbill with a distinctive pale blue eye-ring and short, deep bill, found in bamboo and scrub of the eastern Himalayas and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Suthora nipalensis
children.description: A small parrotbill with a black throat and spectacled face pattern, inhabiting bamboo thickets in the Himalayas and montane Southeast Asia.

children.name: Sylvia
children.description: Scrub warblers of hedgerows, thickets, and forest edge. The Blackcap is one of Sweden's finest songsters, a rich outpouring of clear fluted notes; the Common Whitethroat perches conspicuously on bramble tops to deliver its scratchy, cheerful song.

children.name: Sylvia abyssinica
children.description: A skulking warbler-like bird of African montane forest understorey, recently moved into Sylvia from the babblers.

children.name: Sylvia althaea
children.description: A nondescript grey warbler of open montane scrub and stony slopes in Central Asia, wintering on the Indian subcontinent; reliably told from Lesser Whitethroat only by voice and range.

children.name: Sylvia atricapilla
children.description: One of Sweden's finest songsters, the male's rich fluty warble pouring from woodland and gardens, the sexes told apart by black (male) or chestnut (female) caps. Increasing numbers now winter in Britain rather than the Mediterranean.

children.name: Sylvia balearica
children.description: A grey scrub warbler endemic to the Balearic Islands, closely related to Marmora's Warbler.

children.name: Sylvia borin
children.description: A plain, featureless brown warbler whose anonymity is offset by a sustained, mellow, babbling song from dense scrub and young woodland. A long-distance migrant wintering in sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Sylvia cantillans
children.description: A slim Mediterranean warbler, the male with a brick-red throat bordered by a white moustache, breeding in open woodland and scrub.

children.name: Sylvia communis
children.description: A restless warbler of brambly hedgerows and field margins, the male delivering a scratchy song from an exposed perch or in a brief dancing song-flight. It winters in the African Sahel.

children.name: Sylvia conspicillata
children.description: A small, rufous-winged warbler of low coastal scrub and saltmarsh around the Mediterranean and Macaronesia.

children.name: Sylvia crassirostris
children.description: A large pale-eyed warbler of the eastern Mediterranean and Middle East, wintering in northeast Africa and Arabia.

children.name: Sylvia curruca
children.description: A small, neat warbler with a dark mask and a distinctive song that ends in a dry rattle, skulking in tall hedges and thickets. It migrates south-east towards north-east Africa via the eastern Mediterranean.

children.name: Sylvia deserti
children.description: A pale sandy-coloured warbler of arid stony plains and desert scrub across the Sahel and northern Africa, formerly treated as a subspecies of the Asian Desert Warbler.

children.name: Sylvia deserticola
children.description: A warbler of North African mountain scrub and Atlas woodland, the male warm rufous below.

children.name: Sylvia dohrni
children.description: A plump, thrush-like warbler endemic to the islands of São Tomé, skulking in forest understorey with a rich, whistled song.

children.name: Sylvia galinieri
children.description: A long-tailed, cat-like warbler of Ethiopian highland forests, its song a sustained medley of mimicry and musical phrases; named after the French naturalist Joseph Galinier.

children.name: Sylvia halimodendri
children.description: A pale, sandy-grey warbler breeding in Central Asian shrub desert and wintering in the Middle East and northeast Africa.

children.name: Sylvia hortensis
children.description: A large, pale-eyed warbler of open Mediterranean woodland and olive groves, with a black-capped male and rich fluty song.

children.name: Sylvia leucomelaena
children.description: A black-capped warbler of acacia scrub in the southern Levant, Arabia, and northeast Africa, often cocking and dipping its tail.

children.name: Sylvia margelanica
children.description: A distinct pale whitethroat of riverine thickets and scrub in Central Asian valleys, wintering in southern Asia.

children.name: Sylvia melanocephala
children.description: A common resident warbler of Mediterranean scrub, the male with a glossy black cap, red eye-ring, and white throat; one of the most widespread breeders of southern Europe.

children.name: Sylvia melanothorax
children.description: A scrub warbler endemic as a breeder to Cyprus, the male heavily spotted black below; winters in the eastern Mediterranean and Middle East.

children.name: Sylvia minula
children.description: A small, pale whitethroat of Central Asian desert and semi-desert, with a more delicate bill than the Lesser Whitethroat.

children.name: Sylvia mystacea
children.description: A scrub warbler of the Caucasus, Middle East, and Central Asia, the male with a pinkish flush and dark cap; winters in northeast Africa.

children.name: Sylvia nana
children.description: A tiny, pale sandy-coloured warbler of arid plains and scrub, constantly flicking its tail and wings as it forages on the ground.

children.name: Sylvia nigricapillus
children.description: A dark-capped warbler-like babbler of South African coastal scrub and thickets, now placed in the Sylvia genus.

children.name: Sylvia nisoria
children.description: A large, heavy warbler with a fierce yellow eye and barred underparts in the adult, often nesting near Red-backed Shrikes. It reaches the western edge of its range in south-east Sweden.

children.name: Sylvia rueppelli
children.description: A scrub warbler of the eastern Mediterranean, the male with a black throat and crown separated by a white moustachial stripe.

children.name: Sylvia sarda
children.description: A grey, long-tailed Mediterranean scrub warbler breeding on Corsica, Sardinia, and nearby islands.

children.name: Sylvia subalpina
children.description: A Mediterranean warbler split from the Subalpine Warbler, breeding on Corsica, Sardinia, and northwest Italy, identified by its distinctive rattling call.

children.name: Sylvia undata
children.description: A small, dark, long-tailed warbler of lowland heath and Mediterranean scrub; northern populations crash in hard winters. Near Threatened in parts of its range.
